title: Service Unreachable — Nothing on Port 8080
scenario: You have been handed access to a server that is supposed to be running a web service on port 8080. The on-call engineer reported 'connection refused' and escalated to you. Nothing has been started yet — the outage IS the starting state. Your job is to confirm the diagnosis methodically and then bring the service back up.
symptoms: No process is listening on port 8080
ss -tulnp shows an empty listener table for port 8080
mission: Use curl to confirm connection refused, then use ss -tulnp to verify nothing is listening. Write your diagnosis to /home/intern/diagnosis.txt (include the phrase 'nothing listening' or similar). Then start the web server with 'python3 -m http.server 8080 &' and verify with curl.

hints.title: Confirm the failure first
hints.content: Run 'curl localhost:8080'. You will see 'Connection refused'. This is the error we need to diagnose.

hints.title: Inspect the socket table
hints.content: Run 'ss -tulnp'. Look at the Local Address:Port column. Is there any entry for port 8080? The flags mean: -t TCP, -u UDP, -l listening, -n numeric, -p show process.

hints.title: Write your diagnosis
hints.content: Run: echo "No process listening on port 8080" > /home/intern/diagnosis.txt

hints.title: Start the service
hints.content: Run: python3 -m http.server 8080 &  — the & sends it to the background so your shell stays usable.

hints.title: Verify the fix
hints.content: Run 'ss -tulnp' again — you should now see :8080 listed. Then 'curl localhost:8080' should return an HTML page (HTTP 200).

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/diagnosis.txt exists and contains the diagnosis keyword
A process is now listening on port 8080
